svg.title: The data role landscape: 15 roles grouped by seven core families, ordered from far to close on the decision arc
svg.desc: A portrait grid mapping fifteen data roles against seven skill categories (build, architect, govern, analyze, translate, domain, authority). Rows are grouped into seven core families and ordered top-to-bottom by decision arc position — far from the decision at top, close at bottom. A right-edge indicator marks the arc direction with a caveat about contexts where it may collapse.
svg.text: Reference map — 15 roles across seven skills, grouped into seven families
Director of Data Strategy & Decision Support
Directional shape. May collapse in real-time systems, embedded ML, or heavy compliance contexts where engineering, governance, or compliance IS the decision layer.
The composite role many JDs imply
heavy in every column at once — the shape no real role fits
The seven layers of the data-and-AI stack
Where the skills above live in the pipeline, bottom to top

g.text: Cloud, network, storage. DevOps / SRE / platform.
Ingest, move, transform. Data + Analytics Engineers.
Schemas, semantic layer, MDM, entity resolution.
BI, insights, decision science, impact measurement.

svg.text: Compliance is external and regulatory (HIPAA, FERPA, SOC2, GLBA). Governance is internal — semantic layer,
definitional authority, decision frameworks, stewardship. A JD naming HIPAA specifically may leave governance abstract.
